skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound.
skill.summary: Create professional AI videos using 40+ state-of-the-art models through a unified CLI interface for content creators and marketers.
skill.author: inference-sh-8

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 65 patterns, but all are false positives from documentation content. The external_commands detections (43 locations) are CLI usage examples in markdown, not executable code. Network detections (19 URLs) are documentation links. The skill restricts bash access to infsh commands only via allowed-tools directive. One low-severity finding for curl-to-shell installation pattern in documentation, which is standard practice with checksum verification.

low_findings.title: Curl to Shell Installation Pattern
low_findings.description: Documentation includes curl piped to sh for CLI installation. While this is standard practice for CLI tools and the script verifies checksums, users should be aware this pattern downloads and executes remote code.

content.value_statement: Creating professional videos requires expensive software and editing skills. This skill provides instant access to 40+ AI video models through simple CLI commands for content creation.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo 3.1, Grok, and other models
Animate static images into videos with Wan 2.5 and Seedance models
Create AI avatars with lip-sync using OmniHuman and Fabric
Upscale video quality using Topaz AI upscaler
Add sound effects and foley audio to silent videos
Merge multiple video clips with transition effects
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and API account with credits
Video generation is asynchronous - results are not instant
Cannot generate videos longer than model-specific duration limits
Output quality depends on prompt clarity and model capabilities

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams create engaging video content for social platforms without video production expertise or equipment.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product Demo Videos
use_cases.description: E-commerce businesses generate product showcase videos from images and descriptions for online stores.

use_cases.title: Explainer Video Production
use_cases.description: Educators and trainers create animated explainer videos with AI avatars for online courses and tutorials.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second video of a drone flying over a mountain landscape at sunset with cinematic lighting
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Simple video generation with Veo

prompt_templates.title: Image Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this portrait image by adding subtle head movement and blinking. Use Wan 2.5 for natural motion.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Converting static images to video

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with Narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking avatar video using this headshot and audio file. The avatar should lip-sync naturally to the speech with appropriate facial expressions.

prompt_templates.title: Complete Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a product demo video: first create the base video from my script, add foley sound effects matching the scene, upscale to 1080p, then merge with an intro clip using fade transition.

output_examples.input: Generate a timelapse video of flowers blooming in a garden
output_examples.output: Video file (MP4, 5 seconds, 720p) showing accelerated flower blooming sequence with natural lighting changes

output_examples.input: Animate this landscape photo with flowing water and moving clouds
output_examples.output: Animated video (MP4, 8 seconds) with natural water flow motion and cloud movement while keeping foreground elements stable

best_practices: Write specific, descriptive prompts including lighting, camera angle, and motion details for better results
Start with shorter duration videos to test prompts before generating longer content
Use image-to-video when you need consistent character or scene appearance across multiple videos
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like make a cool video without specific visual details
Do not expect photorealistic human faces - current models may produce artifacts
Avoid requesting copyrighted characters or celebrity likenesses in generated videos

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh in your terminal, then run infsh login to authenticate with your account.

faq.question: Which video generation model should I use?
faq.answer: Google Veo 3.1 offers the best overall quality. For faster results use Veo 3.1 Fast. For image animation, Wan 2.5 provides excellent results.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model and length. Short videos (5-8 seconds) typically complete in 1-5 minutes. Longer videos may take 10+ minutes.

faq.question: Can I use my own images for video generation?
faq.answer: Yes, upload your image to a publicly accessible URL and provide it in the image_url parameter for image-to-video models.

faq.question: What video formats are supported for output?
faq.answer: Videos are generated in MP4 format. Resolution varies by model from 720p to 1080p depending on the selected model.

faq.question: How do I add sound to my generated videos?
faq.answer: Some models like Veo support audio generation. For other videos, use the foley tool to add sound effects, or merge with separate audio using the media merger.
